name: Texas Poppy-Sweet Cake (Low Fat)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Tub-style canola or corn oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Coat a 12-cup bundt pan with non-stick spray.
3. In a large mixing bowl, beat margarine and sugar together using electric beaters until smooth.
4. Add buttermilk, poppy-seed filling, egg substitute, vanilla, and sour cream. Beat until well combined.
5. Gradually add flour, baking soda, and salt with a spoon until just mixed.
6. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites until stiff but not dry.
7. Gently fold the beaten egg whites into the batter using a spatula.
8. Pour the batter evenly into the prepared pan.
9. Bake in the preheated oven for about 1 hour and 10 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
10. Allow cooling in the pan for 10 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely.
11. Dust with powdered sugar if desired.
This recipe makes 16 servings. Original recipe contains approximately: 379 calories, 91 mg cholesterol, 18 g fat, and 359 mg sodium. Revised recipe contains approximately: 298 calories, negligible cholesterol, 9 g fat, and 334 mg sodium.
